title: The Cyclic (465,145,45) Difference-Set Problem
lean_gate_reason: Lean would only formalize a conditional orbit-contraction slice. The publication bottleneck is earlier: the packet still lacks a source-backed bridge from the exact cyclic problem to the claimed 2-multiplier symmetry, or an alternative multiplier-free proof of the unconditional theorem.
micro_paper_assessment: The packet preserves a real conditional obstruction, but it does not close the selected frontier claim. Its strongest honest output is a lemma-sized impossibility result for 2-orbit-constant contractions modulo 93 and 155 under an unsupported 2-multiplier premise, so it fails the one-shot micro-paper lane and should be moved aside unless that bridge is repaired exactly.
family_affinity: strong residual cyclic difference-set case in the small-parameter literature
generalization_signal: the orbit-compressed contraction template looks reusable, but the decisive bridge from n = 100 to unconditional 2-symmetry is missing and does not currently scale
strongest_honest_claim: If one additionally assumes that multiplication by 2 can be imposed as a genuine multiplier symmetry for a cyclic (465,145,45) difference set, then no 2-orbit-constant contracted coefficient vector modulo 93 or 155 satisfies the exact sum, square-sum, and nonzero-correlation equations.
paper_title_hint: Conditional Orbit-Contraction Obstructions to a Cyclic (465,145,45) Difference Set
candidate_theorem_slice: No 2-orbit-constant contraction modulo 93 or 155 satisfies the exact coefficient equations for a hypothetical cyclic (465,145,45) difference set, provided the missing 2-multiplier symmetry premise is justified.
